def: Muddy; slimy; thick. Sir T. Browne.
A plane of an orthorhombic crystal which is parallel both tothe vertical axis and to the shorter lateral (brachydiagonal) axis.
Having masts smaller than the usual dimension; -- said ofvessels. Totten.
The act of preconceiving; conception or opinion previouslyformed.
Not defective; complete; as, an acatalectic verse.-- n.
To climb up; to ascend.Upclomb the shadowy pine above the woven copse. Tennyson.
To gain sight of; to discover at a distance, or in a state ofconcealment; to espy; to see.One in reading, skipped over all sentences where he spied a note ofadmiration. Swift.
Pert. to, or caused by, electro-capillarity.
Consisting or partaking of marl; resembling marl; aboundingwith marl.
